data.description:
## Why
Code review persists NOTHING to the shared store today: the reviewbot only has ephemeral gate snapshots
under /tmp with no origin remote and no GitHub push credential (verified on the box). So there is no
durable record and no corpus to calibrate the new impact model from.

## What
Emit a change-scoped `code_review` artifact keyed by (gerrit-change-id, revision), carrying the impact +
tier-tagged sub-answers + `norm_id`/`material_fingerprint` join keys, and `relates_to` EVERY rebar ticket
cited by the change's commit trailers (many-to-one; the session_log pattern - out of the dependency graph,
excluded from Jira sync, searchable). Provision the reviewbot with a PERSISTENT pushable tickets store and
the RebarBotNava PAT at `/rebar/prod/reviewbot-tickets-pat` (SSM -> .env -> git credential helper on origin;
never on-disk plaintext), `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`, and wire the emission path. The PAT push capability is
already confirmed working from the reviewbot container.

## Scope
- Reviewbot infra (`docker-compose.yml`, `Dockerfile.reviewbot`, an SSM materialize step for the PAT +
  a persistent store) and the emission code (`code_review` artifact type/handling + the emit call).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A `code_review` artifact type/handling exists: keyed by change+revision, kept OUT of the dependency
      graph, excluded from Jira sync, and searchable (session_log-style).
- [ ] The reviewbot has a persistent tickets store whose `origin` authenticates via the PAT sourced from
      SSM `.env` (not on disk); `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`.
- [ ] On a real review the artifact is written and lands on `origin/tickets`, linked `relates_to` to all
      trailer-cited tickets; unresolved/foreign trailer refs are logged, not fatal.
- [ ] No retention cap (deliberate).
data.title: Code-review data capture to the rebar ticket store (change-scoped, PAT-credentialed)
